messages.content:
You are an expert Technical Product Manager.
Your job is to analyze the user's issue (Title and Body) along with the provided source code context, and generate a User Story, Acceptance Criteria, and Tasks.

OUTPUT FORMAT REQUIREMENTS:
You MUST output ONLY a valid JSON object. Do not wrap it in markdown. Do not include any explanations outside of the JSON.
The JSON must strictly follow this structure:
{
  "title": "Short title of the story",
  "story_type": "software_feature", 
  "jira_issue_type": "Story",
  "jira_labels": ["backend", "frontend", "bug", etc],
  "jira_linked_items": [],
  "user_story": "As a [role], I want to [action] so that [benefit].",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Given [precondition] When [action] Then [result]"
  ],
  "story_points": null, // Hoặc một số nguyên Fibonacci (1, 2, 3, 5, 8) đánh giá độ khó
  "tasks": {
    "be": ["Backend task 1", "Backend task 2"],
    "fe": ["Frontend task 1"],
    "qa": ["Test case 1"]
  },
  "definition_of_done": ["Code reviewed", "Tests passed"],
  "planning_status": "READY_FOR_SPRINT",
  "clarification_questions": [],
  "assumptions": [],
  "story_splits": [],
  "sprint_allocation": [],
  "context_sources": []
}

If the issue lacks so much context that it's impossible to deduce the required changes even with the provided code context, you may set "planning_status" to "NEEDS_CLARIFICATION" and provide your questions in "clarification_questions", leaving "tasks" empty. Otherwise, strive to generate concrete tasks.

ISSUE TITLE: Disabled plugin is used by agent ISSUE BODY: I disabled the model-council plugin, but it's still getting used by the agent host. [ah-logs-Remove-agentHostSessionListController.ts.zip](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/29153090/ah-logs-Remove-agentHostSessionListController.ts.zip) I think I installed it from vscode, the dialog is confusing. I might be wrong though, can't remember. --- RAG CONTEXT (SOURCE CODE) --- --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- # Agent Plugins Architecture Agent plugins are a modular extension system that allows external packages of prompts, hooks, skills, agents, and MCP server definitions to be discovered, installed, and contributed into the chat experience. This document describes the architecture of the `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/common/plugins/` layer and its browser-side implementations. --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- #### Verified SDK facts (read from `node_modules/@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk/sdk.d.ts`) - **`enabledPlugins`** (`sdk.d.ts` L4899): `{ [pluginId]: string[] | boolean | { [k]: unknown } }`. Id format is `plugin-id@marketplace-id`. A plugin is **enabled** when its value is `true`, a `string[]` (version constraint), or an object (extended form); **disabled** only when `false`. **Precedence is `user < project < local`** (later scope wins) — so `false` in `.claude/settings.local.json` disables a plugin that `.claude/settings.json` or `~/.claude/settings.json` enabled. This is the **opposite** of the hook scanner's "first-seen-wins" dedupe (Decision PB-1). - **`system/init.plugins`** (`sdk.d.ts` L3943): `{ name: string; path: string }[]` — name + path only (no `source`). This is the post-materialize source of truth for "which native plugins did the live session actually load". --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- # Agent Host The agent host communicates via the Agent Host Protocol. The specification for this lives in a directory `../agent-host-protocol` as a sibling of the VS Code directory. If this directory doesn't exist, you should use the "ask questions" tool to ask the user if they want to clone `git@github.com:microsoft/agent-host-protocol.git` to that directory. After doing so, you should also prompt the user to add `file:////plugins/copilot-plugin` as a plugin in their `chat.pluginLocations` settings. --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- ### Browser layer (`browser/`) | File | Role | |------|------| | `agentPluginRepositoryService.ts` | Browser implementation of `IAgentPluginRepositoryService` (git clone/pull via Git service) | | `pluginSources.ts` | Concrete `IPluginSource` implementations: `GitHubPluginSource`, `GitUrlPluginSource`, `NpmPluginSource`, `PipPluginSource`, `RelativePathPluginSource` | | `pluginInstallService.ts` | Browser implementation of `IPluginInstallService` | | `agentPluginsView.ts` | Installed-plugins tree view UI | | `agentPluginActions.ts` | Context-menu actions for plugins | | `agentPluginEditor/` | Rich editor for browsing a single plugin's contents | --- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md --- ### IAgentPluginService Main entry point consumed by the rest of the workbench: ``` plugins – IObservable (aggregate of all ready discoveries, sorted by URI) enablementModel – Shared IEnablementModel for plugin enable/disable state ``` Registered as `InstantiationType.Delayed` — only instantiated when first injected.

response:
```json
{
  "title": "Fix disabled plugin still being loaded by agent host",
  "story_type": "software_feature", 
  "jira_issue_type": "Bug",
  "jira_labels": ["agent-host", "plugin-system", "bug", "session-management"],
  "jira_linked_items": [],
  "user_story": "As a user, I want disabled plugins to be excluded from agent host sessions so that the agent doesn't use features or tools from plugins I've explicitly turned off.",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Given a plugin is marked as disabled in enabledPlugins configuration When an agent session starts Then the disabled plugin should NOT appear in system/init.plugins",
    "Given multiple plugins with different enablement states (enabled, partially enabled, disabled) When initializing an agent host session Then only plugins matching their specific enablement criteria should be loaded",
    "Given a user disables a plugin via VS Code settings When they start a new chat session Then the agent host must respect this disable state and not load the plugin",
    "Given the agent host protocol specification exists Then system/init.plugins must conform to the expected format from the protocol spec"
  ],
  "story_points": 5,
  "tasks": {
    "be": [
      "Investigate current flow of enabledPlugins -> system/init.plugins transformation",
      "Identify where disabled plugins are incorrectly included in init.plugins",
      "Add filtering logic to exclude disabled plugins from session initialization",
      "Ensure enablementModel state is properly synchronized with plugin loading decisions"
    ],
    "fe": [
      "Verify agentPluginsView correctly reflects enabled/disabled states",
      "Test that disable action persists across sessions"
    ],
    "qa": [
      "Create test case: Disable model-council plugin, start new session, verify it's not loaded",
      "Create test case: Enable/Disable toggle multiple times, verify state persistence",
      "Verify agent host protocol compliance for init.plugins format"
    ]
  },
  "definition_of_done": [
    "Code reviewed and approved",
    "All acceptance criteria verified through testing",
    "Agent host sessions no longer load disabled plugins",
    "Documentation updated to reflect plugin enablement behavior"
  ],
  "planning_status": "READY_FOR_SPRINT",
  "clarification_questions": [
    "Is there a specific agent host session where this is occurring, or all sessions?",
    "What version of the agent-host-protocol are we using for init.plugins specification?",
    "Are there any known workarounds users can apply to bypass this issue?"
  ],
  "assumptions": [
    "The enabledPlugins configuration in .claude/settings.json is being read correctly",
    "The disconnect is between enablement state and session initialization, not the disable action itself",
    "system/init.plugins should be filtered based on enabledPlugins state before materialization"
  ],
  "story_splits": [
    {
      "name": "Backend: Fix plugin filtering logic",
      "priority": "high",
      "description": "Implement the core fix to filter disabled plugins from init.plugins"
    },
    {
      "name": "Frontend: Verify disable state persistence",
      "priority": "medium", 
      "description": "Ensure users can reliably disable plugins and see it persist across sessions"
    }
  ],
  "sprint_allocation": [
    {"task": "Backend filtering implementation", "story_points": 5, "assignee": ""},
    {"task": "QA test cases", "story_points": 2, "assignee": ""}
  ],
  "context_sources": [
    "vscode_ai_docs_full.md - Agent Plugins Architecture",
    "vscode_ai_docs_full.md - Verified SDK facts (enabledPlugins)",
    "vscode_ai_docs_full.md - Agent Host Protocol documentation"
  ]
}
```
